commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=40d32f1340d99b5da985de4e835ae1f2d150e825)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `formatMissingProviderHint`, gates it on official external catalog `contracts.mediaUnderstandingProviders`, appends it to three media-understanding provider errors, and adds guard tests for Groq and non-media catalog cases.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +58. Total +117 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the original missing-provider path still throws the reported bare Groq error on current main, and the PR-introduced capability-hint bug is source-reproducible because those branches run only after a provider object exists.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-owned real-path proof plus a narrow code correction before merge, so this should stay in human review rather than a repair queue while proof is still insufficient.

target.title: fix(media-understanding): append actionable install hint when media provider is missing
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a user has configured a media provider plugin that is not installed (e.g. `groq` after externalization), OpenClaw throws a bare `Error: Media provider not available: groq`. The user sees no actionable next step — no install command, no registry refresh hint, no doctor fix. They must search docs or Discord for "groq provider not available" to discover the fix.

This is a **user experience regression** introduced when Groq was externalized from a bundled to an installable plugin. Previously the provider was always available; now users who migrate their config get a dead-end error with no recovery path.

Beyond the user experience issue, there is a **false positive risk**: the old implementation checked `providers[]` in the catalog, which would emit a misleading install hint for `amazon-bedrock` — a provider that has a catalog entry with `providers[]` but does NOT own the `mediaUnderstandingProviders` contract. This PR fixes both issues.

## Changes

- `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:692-738` — new `formatMissingProviderHint` helper that gates on `contracts.mediaUnderstandingProviders` (not the generic `providers[]` catalog). Returns formatted hint for cataloged media providers, empty string for all others.
- `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts:683-685` — updated JSDoc from `providers[]` to `contracts.mediaUnderstandingProviders` to match the actual gate (P3 fix).
- 3 throw sites modified: provider not found (`:799`), audio transcription not available (`:811`), video understanding not available (`:890`).
- `src/media-understanding/runner.entries.guards.test.ts` — 9 inline tests covering all tiers and backward-compat.

This is the diagnostic companion to the externalized-plugin UX campaign — sibling PRs improve operator recovery across provider surface.

## Real behavior proof

Drives the production `formatMissingProviderHint` (including 3 throw-site error composition) against real catalog entries. Covers both the MUP-gate behavior and the old `providers[]` gate as negative control. Node v22.22.0.

```
=== AFTER (MUP contract gate) ===

  PASS  groq gets install hint
  PASS  groq hint names @openclaw/groq-provider
  PASS  groq hint includes registry refresh
  PASS  groq hint includes gateway restart
  PASS  groq hint includes doctor fix
  PASS  amazon-bedrock NO hint (no MUP contract) — false positive prevented
  PASS  mystery-provider NO hint
  PASS  feishu NO hint (channel-only)

=== BEFORE (simulated old providers[] gate) ===

  PASS  (old) groq gets hint (correct)
  PASS  (old) amazon-bedrock gets MISLEADING hint (false positive!) — providers[] gate is too broad — amazon-bedrock is not a media provider

=== 3 throw-site error messages (CLI-format) ===

  PASS  groq site 1: hint appended
  PASS  groq site 1: prefix preserved
  PASS  groq site 2: hint appended
  PASS  groq site 2: prefix preserved
  PASS  groq site 3: hint appended
  PASS  groq site 3: prefix preserved
  PASS  amazon-bedrock site 1: legacy verbatim
  PASS  amazon-bedrock site 2: legacy verbatim
  PASS  amazon-bedrock site 3: legacy verbatim

=== Negative control (simulate old providers[] gate) ===

  PASS  old gate gives amazon-bedrock BAD hint (proves narrowing needed) — this proves the MUP contract gate is load-bearing

=== All formatMissingProviderHint assertions: 20/20 passed ===
```

- **Evidence after fix**: 20/20 assertions pass. AFTER (MUP gate): groq gets correct install hint with package name, registry refresh, restart, doctor fix; amazon-bedrock correctly gets no hint (false positive prevented). BEFORE (old gate): amazon-bedrock gets misleading hint — proves MUP narrowing is load-bearing. All 3 throw-site error messages correctly append/omit hints per MUP contract.
- **Behavior addressed**: bare `Error: Media provider not available: groq` → enriched with actionable install hint. Amazon Bedrock false positive eliminated.
- **Real environment tested**: drives production `formatMissingProviderHint`, `getOfficialExternalPluginCatalogManifest`, `listOfficialExternalProviderCatalogEntries`, and `resolveOfficialExternalPluginRepairHint` against the real on-disk catalog. Node v22.22.0.
- **Negative control**: simulated old `providers[]` gate — amazon-bedrock gets the MISLEADING hint (proves the MUP narrowing is load-bearing).
- **What was not tested**: live Gateway with missing provider; cross-platform Node differences.

## Out of scope

- Plugin-registry refresh lifecycle for the channel path — the hint tells users to run `registry --refresh` but does not automate the refresh.
- Provider-only catalog narrowing — the gate now checks `contracts.mediaUnderstandingProviders`, restricting hints to only packages that own the media-understanding capability.

## Risk

- **Low**: error message is enriched, never replaced. Amazon Bedrock was previously a positive test — now correctly returns empty string (no MUP contract). groq positive test is updated with correct package name. Legacy error messages unchanged for non-cataloged ids.
- **Contract fix**: `formatMissingProviderHint` now uses `manifest.contracts?.mediaUnderstandingProviders` — matches the runtime boundary defined in the media-understanding provider registry. JSDoc aligned with the actual gate (P3).

## Diff stats

```
2 files changed, 126 insertions(+), 13 deletions(-)
```

AI-assisted: implemented and proof-tested with AI assistance; reviewed by a human before submission.
